program_name: Mathematics, Secondary Education with Initial Licensure (8-12), B.A.
faculty: Chairperson Peter Staab Professors Associate Professors Assistant Professors Mary Ann Barbato Nestor Diaz Morera Nermin Bayazit Jessica Oehrlein Catherine Buell Gerald Higdon Peter Staab Amy Wehe Sarah Wright

requirement_sections.heading: Mathematics Program
content: The Department of Mathematics serves all students at the university. Mathematics majors receive a comprehensive foundation in abstract and applied mathematics as preparation for graduate school or a professional career. Minors in Mathematics receive the mathematical foundation needed for advanced work in their major field.
The department also provides non-majors with courses for their major or with courses for their General Education program.
Develop effective thinking and communication skills
Learn to link applications and theory
Develop mathematical independence and experience open-ended inquiry
items: Present information in a clear, precise and organized manner both verbally and in writing.
Use and compare analytical, visual, and numerical perspectives in exploring mathematics.
Recognize and make mathematically rigorous arguments.
Approach mathematical problems with curiosity and creativity and persist in the face of difficulties.
Work creatively and self-sufficiently with mathematics.
Understand and apply motivating examples that illustrate the ideas they are studying.
Apply mathematical ideas to problems in those areas of study.
See mathematical theory as useful and enlightening in both pure and applied contexts.
Recognize and integrate connections between mathematical courses and theory.
Use technology effectively, both as a tool for solving problems and exploring mathematical ideas.
Use technology with increasing sophistication throughout a major curriculum.
Be able to explore mathematical ideas and problems beyond the classroom.
Explore increasingly more difficult and open-ended questions.
Speak and write about mathematics with increasing depth and sophistication.

requirement_sections.heading: Language Requirement
content: The B.A. requires foreign language proficiency at the intermediate level.

requirement_sections.heading: Advanced Requirements for Mathematics Secondary Education
content: These courses are designed to give a breadth of knowledge in the three areas: Theoretical Mathematics, Applied and Statistics. These requirements also ensure a student has sufficient exposure to the high level courses.
The following courses (with the advanced group they are in) are required for content requirements from DESE and still fit the breadth and depth requirements of all other mathematics concentrations.
items: Theoretical Courses
Applied Courses
Statistics Course

requirement_sections.heading: Mathematics with Initial Licensure Concentration
content: Students within our Mathematics major can pursue initial licensure as high school Mathematics teachers. This program provides students with both a broad introduction to high school teaching and specific instruction in the theory, research and practice of secondary Mathematics teaching. Students engage in field-based experiences in the school setting supervised by our faculty through on-site pre-practicum experiences coupled with each teaching course and a formal teaching practicum as the capstone experience. Students pursuing this option will have to fulfill the degree requirements for the mathematics major with some restricted electives, a minor in Middle and Secondary Education (5-12), as well as the requirements for initial teacher licensure in Secondary Education.The requirements are described below:
These courses are required for subject matter knowledge requirements for DESE.
items: These courses are required for subject matter knowledge requirements for DESE.
Middle and Secondary Education (5-12) Minor
